name: Doris's Pralines
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix all ingredients except butter, vanilla, and pecans.
2. Cook on low heat until the mixture forms a soft ball when dropped in cold water.
3. Remove from heat. Add butter, vanilla, and pecans; mix until the mixture holds its shape.
4. Spoon onto buttered wax paper (place old newspaper under the wax paper).
5. If the candy becomes too hard before all is spooned out, add a little water and continue heating gently. Alternatively, let it stand on low heat while spooning out.
6. If you have never tasted freshly made pralines made from fresh Louisiana pecans, you have not truly lived.
Justin Wilson says, "Doris is the much better half (wife) of my good friend Gordon Martin, the sheriff of St. James Parish. Every Christmas Eve we go by to see them after watching the bonfires that are burned on the levees along the Mississippi River to light the way for Papa Noel (Santa Claus).
